figures.caption: Table 1: Few-shot classification performance (mean over 3 seeds) for training-free methods on 11 datasets using CLIP-ResNet-50. Best results in each group are in bold and second best underlined.
Fig. 12: Analysis of the sensitivity of the hyper-parameters λ and α to the validation set accuracy on 4 datasets with CLIP ResNet50.

Fig. 1: Given the classification problem, the image latent space should be differently exploited. The semantic space defined by the text prototypes defines a relevant subspace. We propose text-aligned semantic projection of image features which performs classification using only the relevant subspace. Task relevant regions denoted by attention maps are obtained by the proposed task-specific semantic projection P1 and P2.
Fig. 8: The impact of Align, Mix and Align+Mix prototypes on 9 datasets with CLIP ViT-B/16.
Fig. 10: Performance comparison of GDA [34] with the proposed TAMP and TAMP+LDA classifiers on 9 datasets with CLIP ViT-B/16.
Table 2: Performance comparison with training-based methods using CLIP ViT-B/16 backbone. Best results in each group denoted in bold and second best is underlined.

Fig. 3: Attention maps computed using the ResNet-50 vision encoder of CLIP showing that the text-aligned features focus on the task-relevant information from the image and the orthogonal part on remaining parts of image.
Fig. 6: The impact of different classifiers on 9 datasets with CLIP-ResNet-50.
Fig. 11: Performance comparison of GDA [34] with the proposed TAMP and TAMP+LDA classifiers on 9 datasets with CLIP ResNet50.
